raw_consumer_statement: In XXXX I called my Citi card to dispute a charge ( XXXX ) for merchandise I had received that was of poor quality with the package damaged. The person ( whose name I dont have right now ) told me that since I started a dispute, I could throw away the purchase, sell it, do what I wanted with it, when I inquired about returning it. I had taken pictures of the poor quality and damaged packaging and she did not ask for any of that. I had not been made aware that the dispute was not found in my favor until I saw my credit card had reinstated the purchase in late XXXX. I called on XX/XX/year> spoke to XXXX and she said we verified the purchase and you made no good faith effort to return the items. There was no ability for me to find out who or how to return the items because of how complicated the correspondence was so I asked, should I return the items now? She said I should so I immediate went to the post office and returned them. I then complained about the prior employee not gathering the information I had about the poor product etc and she had me email all of the photos I had including the photo of what I thought I was getting. I sent those to XXXX. I have a receipt that the items were received by the distributor on XX/XX/year>. I called Citi again on XX/XX/year> and spoke to XXXX and then XXXX who basically was of no help whatsoever. I feel like I have been scammed by the vendor and my credit card. I am hoping you can help me get my money back.
